subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
- **Modular space board** (10 double-sided sector tiles arranged to form the galaxy)
- **14 faction boards** (each faction associated with a planet type, with unique abilities and building tracks)
- **Wooden structures** per faction: Mines, Trading Stations, Research Labs, Planetary Institutes, Academies
- **Gaiaformers** (special tokens for colonizing Transdimensional planets)
- **Resources**: Ore (brown), Credits (gold), Knowledge (green), Q.I.C. tokens (Quantum Intelligence Cubes)
- **Power tokens** (cycling through 3 areas on faction board, plus the Gaia area)
- **Research board** (6 research tracks replacing Terra Mystica's cult tracks)
- **Technology tiles** (standard and advanced — gained by reaching level milestones on research tracks)
- **Round scoring tiles** (6 tiles, one per round)
- **Round boosters** (selected each round, provide income and pass bonuses)
- **Federation tokens** (formed from clusters of buildings, like Towns in Terra Mystica)
- **Final scoring tiles** (2 per game, determining end-game bonus VP categories)
- **Action tokens** (for power actions and Q.I.C. actions)
- **Automa cards** (for solo play)

subtopics.title: Board Setup
subtopics.content:
1. Arrange the **10 sector tiles** to form the galaxy map. Each tile shows various planet types and empty space. Use a suggested layout or create your own.
2. Place the **Research board** nearby showing 6 research tracks: Terraforming, Navigation, Artificial Intelligence, Gaiaforming, Economy, and Science.
3. Place **Technology tiles** on the research board — standard tech tiles on level 3-4 positions, advanced tech tiles on level 5 positions.
4. Shuffle and place **6 Round scoring tiles** (one per round).
5. Lay out **Round boosters** (player count + 3).
6. Select **2 Final scoring tiles** randomly — these determine the end-game bonus VP categories.
7. Place resources, Power tokens, Federation tokens, and action tokens in supplies.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
1. Each player selects a **faction**. The 14 factions are organized into 7 pairs, each associated with a planet type:
   - **Desert**: Hadsch Hallas, Bescods
   - **Swamp**: Taklons, Ambas
   - **Oxide**: Geodens, Bal T'aks
   - **Volcanic**: Firaks, Bescods
   - **Titanium**: Lantids, Ivits
   - **Ice**: Nevlas, Itars
   - **Terra**: Terrans, Xenos
   - (Transdimensional planets are colonized via Gaiaforming)
2. Take your **faction board** — populate it with structures covering income tracks. As you build on the map, you uncover higher income values.
3. Set up your **Power areas** (Area I, II, III) with starting Power tokens per your faction sheet.
4. Take starting resources (Ore, Credits, Knowledge, Q.I.C.) as indicated.
5. In reverse player order, place **2 starting Mines** on planets of your native type on the map.
6. Each player selects a **Round booster** for Round 1 (in reverse player order).

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content:
Gaia Project is the spiritual successor to Terra Mystica, set in space. Fourteen factions compete to colonize a modular galaxy by terraforming planets to their native type and building structures. Structures generate resources, which fund further expansion and research.

Key differences from Terra Mystica:
- **Modular board** instead of a fixed map — every game has a different galaxy layout.
- **Research tracks** (6) replace cult tracks, providing powerful technology tiles and passive bonuses.
- **Power cycle** includes a 4th area (Gaia area) for Gaiaforming.
- **Federations** replace Towns — clusters of structures with sufficient power value can form Federations for tokens worth VP and resources.
- **Q.I.C. tokens** are a special resource used for powerful unique actions.

Over **6 rounds**, players build engines, expand territory, research technologies, and form Federations. The player with the most VP wins.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
Each round follows this structure:

### 1. Income Phase
All players simultaneously collect income based on uncovered tracks on their faction board:
- **Ore**: From Mines.
- **Credits**: From Trading Stations.
- **Knowledge**: From Research Labs.
- **Power**: From Planetary Institutes, Academies, and certain tech tiles.
- **Q.I.C.**: From certain structures and research advances.
- **Round booster income**: Your current booster may provide additional resources.

### 2. Gaia Phase
Resolve any ongoing Gaiaforming projects:
- Power tokens committed to the **Gaia area** return to **Area I**.
- Gaiaformed planets become available for building.

### 3. Action Phase
Players alternate taking **one action** each, clockwise. Continue until all players have passed.

Available actions:

#### a) Build a Mine
- Colonize a planet within **range** (determined by your Navigation research level) of an existing structure.
- If the planet is your native type, build a Mine directly (costs Ore + Credits).
- If it's a different type, you must **terraform** it first (costs Ore based on the number of terraforming steps, modified by your Terraforming research).
- **Transdimensional (purple) planets** require a special Gaiaforming process (see Special Rules).

#### b) Upgrade a Structure
- **Mine → Trading Station** (costs Ore + Credits): Unlocks Credit income. If built adjacent to an opponent's structure, cost is reduced.
- **Trading Station → Research Lab** (costs Ore + Credits): Unlocks Knowledge income and lets you gain a **Technology tile**.
- **Trading Station → Planetary Institute** (costs Ore + Credits): Unique per faction — unlocks your faction's **special ability** and Power income.
- **Research Lab → Academy** (costs Ore + Credits): Gain an **Advanced Technology tile** and Knowledge income.

#### c) Research
- Spend **Knowledge** to advance one step on a **Research track**.
- Each track provides escalating passive bonuses and may grant a **Technology tile** at certain levels.
- Reaching **level 5** on any track grants a powerful **Advanced Technology tile** (limited, first-come-first-served) and a large one-time bonus.

#### d) Power/Q.I.C. Actions
- Spend Power (from Area III) for power actions: gain resources, terraform, or perform special actions.
- Spend Q.I.C. for Q.I.C. actions: gain a free technology tile, score VP for planets of a type, or place a mine ignoring range.

#### e) Special Actions
Activate special actions from Technology tiles, Round boosters, or faction abilities. Each can be used **once per round**.

#### f) Form a Federation
When you have a connected cluster of structures with a total **Power value of at least 7** (and the cluster hasn't already been part of a Federation), claim a **Federation token**.
- Federation tokens provide VP and resources (one-time or ongoing).
- Satellites (Power tokens placed in space between planets) can be used to connect non-adjacent structures for Federation purposes.

#### g) Pass
Return your current **Round booster** and select a new one for next round. Gain the **pass bonus** printed on your returning booster (typically VP based on your board state). You take no more actions this round.

### 4. Clean Up
- Resolve the **Round scoring tile** (VP for specific building or research actions taken this round).
- Advance to the next round.

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
### Terraforming
Planet types are arranged on a transformation wheel (similar to Terra Mystica):
- Count the minimum steps between the target planet type and your native type.
- Each step costs **Ore** (amount depends on your Terraforming research level — higher research = cheaper terraforming).
- At Terraforming research level 0, each step costs 3 Ore. At higher levels, costs decrease.

### Power Cycle
Power tokens move through **4 areas**:
- **Area I → Area II → Area III**: Gaining Power moves tokens along this path.
- **Area III**: Spendable Power. Spent tokens go to Area I.
- **Gaia Area**: Power committed to Gaiaforming goes here during the Gaia Phase, then returns to Area I.
- You may **burn** Power (remove tokens from Area II permanently) to fast-track Power to Area III. This is powerful but permanently reduces your Power pool.

### Leeching Power (Passive Power Gain)
When an opponent builds or upgrades **within range 2** (not just adjacent) of your structures, you may **leech Power** equal to the opponent's building's power value minus 1.
- Leeching costs **1 VP per Power gained minus 1** (so leeching 3 Power costs 2 VP).
- You may choose to leech less or decline entirely.

### Research Tracks
Six research tracks provide escalating bonuses:
1. **Terraforming**: Reduces terraforming Ore costs; level 5 allows terraforming any planet in 1 step.
2. **Navigation**: Increases building range; level 5 grants unlimited range.
3. **Artificial Intelligence**: Grants Q.I.C. income; level 5 provides 4 Q.I.C. per round.
4. **Gaiaforming**: Reduces Gaiaforming Power cost; level 5 makes Gaiaforming free.
5. **Economy**: Grants Credit and Ore income; level 5 provides massive resource income.
6. **Science**: Grants Knowledge income; level 5 provides enormous Knowledge.

Reaching **level 3** on a track grants a **standard Technology tile** (choose from available). Reaching **level 5** grants an **Advanced Technology tile** and a large one-time bonus.

### Technology Tiles
- **Standard**: Provide ongoing income, one-time bonuses, or special actions.
- **Advanced**: Much more powerful — provide large VP, special abilities, or major resource boosts. Only one per research track, first to reach level 5 claims it.

### Federations
Forming Federations is critical for scoring:
- Connected structures (within range 1) with **total Power value of 7+** form a Federation.
- You can use **Satellites** (place Power tokens from your supply on empty space hexes between planets) to extend connections.
- Each Federation grants a **Federation token** with VP and resources.
- Federation tokens are limited by type — choose strategically.
- **Important**: Once structures are part of a Federation, they cannot be counted toward a new Federation. Plan your clusters carefully.

### Q.I.C. Actions
Q.I.C. (Quantum Intelligence Cubes) are a special resource:
- **1 Q.I.C.**: Gain a free standard Technology tile.
- **2 Q.I.C.**: Score 3 VP per planet of a chosen type you have colonized.
- **4 Q.I.C.**: Place a Mine on any unoccupied planet ignoring range requirements.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
### Gaiaforming
Transdimensional (purple/gray) planets cannot be directly terraformed. Instead:
1. Place a **Gaiaformer** on the planet and commit Power tokens to the Gaia area (cost depends on Gaiaforming research level).
2. During the next round's Gaia Phase, the planet becomes a **Gaia planet** (green).
3. You may then build a Mine on it (all factions treat Gaia planets as 1 terraforming step from their native type, costing only the QIC or ore equivalent).

### Lost Planet (Ivits)
The Ivits faction has a unique ability to place a "Lost Planet" space hex, creating new buildable territory.

### Faction Asymmetry
All 14 factions play distinctly:
- **Terrans/Xenos**: Balanced, straightforward. Good for learning.
- **Hadsch Hallas**: Can spend Credits as any other resource. Economic powerhouse.
- **Taklons**: Gain a "brainstone" Power token that provides bonus Power and leech advantages.
- **Geodens**: Gain Q.I.C. when terraforming. Research-focused.
- **Firaks**: Downgrade Research Labs into Trading Stations (unique reverse-upgrade) for extra tech tiles.
- **Nevlas**: Use Power from Area III without spending it (Power stays in Area III). Enormous Power action economy.
- **Ivits**: Start with no Mines — place Space Stations instead. Create Federations differently.
- **Itars**: Can convert burned (removed) Power into Gaiaformers. Unique Power management.
- **Ambas**: Can swap their Planetary Institute with a Mine, leaping across the map.
- **Bal T'aks**: Start without Gaiaformers but gain free research when Gaiaforming becomes available.

### Satellites and Space
When forming Federations, you may place Power tokens on empty space hexes as **Satellites**. These connect non-adjacent structures but cost Power permanently (tokens are removed from your supply). Strategic placement of Satellites is critical for Federation efficiency.

### Range
Your building range starts at 1 (adjacent planets only). Advancing the Navigation research track increases range. At level 5, range is unlimited. Some Q.I.C. actions also bypass range restrictions.

### Final Scoring Tiles
Two Final scoring tiles (selected during setup) determine end-game bonus VP categories. Examples:
- Most structures in Federations.
- Most planet types colonized.
- Most structures on the map.
- Most sectors (board tiles) with at least 1 structure.
- Highest advancement on a research track.

These tiles provide 18/12/6/0 VP for 1st/2nd/3rd/4th place.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
After **Round 6**, final scoring occurs:

| Category | Details |
|---|---|
| **In-game VP** | All VP earned during 6 rounds (building, round scoring tiles, power actions, technology tiles, research bonuses, etc.) |
| **Research Track VP** | Each research track position provides end-game VP based on the level reached (4 VP at level 3, 8 VP at level 4, etc.) |
| **Final Scoring Tiles** | Two categories determined by the Final scoring tiles chosen during setup. 18/12/6/0 VP for 1st/2nd/3rd/4th. |
| **Resources** | Leftover resources convert to VP: every 3 Ore, Credits, or Knowledge = 1 VP. Each Q.I.C. = 1 VP. Power tokens in Area III: every 3 = 1 VP. |

The player with the **most total VP** wins. Ties are broken by most resources remaining.

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Research is king**. Advancing on research tracks provides technology tiles, income, and VP. Prioritize research more than you think you should.
- **Plan Federations from the start**. Your initial Mine placement should set up efficient Federation clusters. Reaching 7 power value is easier with a Planetary Institute (worth 4 power value) in the cluster.
- **Don't spread too thin**. Concentrated building clusters form Federations. Scattered outposts don't.
- **Watch the Round scoring tiles**. Like Terra Mystica, aligning your actions with the current round's scoring bonus is essential.
- **Leech Power aggressively**. The VP cost is usually worth it. Power in Area III is extremely flexible.
- **Use your faction ability early**. Understanding and leveraging your faction's unique power is the biggest skill differentiator.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Research rush**: Reaching level 5 on a research track first claims the Advanced Technology tile (which are extremely powerful). Identify which track aligns with your faction and race for it.
- **Federation planning**: Map out your Federation clusters during setup. A single Planetary Institute (4 power value) + 1 Mine (1) + 1 Trading Station (2) = exactly 7. Add Satellites for flexibility.
- **Final scoring tile alignment**: The two Final scoring tiles are worth up to 36 VP combined. Build your entire strategy around them.
- **Round booster cycling**: Passing early gives you first pick of Round boosters. Some boosters are dramatically more valuable depending on your strategy. The pass bonus VP on boosters can be significant in later rounds.
- **Navigation vs. Terraforming**: Early Navigation research lets you reach more planets cheaply. Early Terraforming research lets you colonize non-native planets cheaply. Choose based on map layout and faction.
- **Power burn timing**: Burning Power (removing from Area II) provides immediate Area III Power but permanently shrinks your pool. Burn early when you need tempo; avoid burning late when your engine should sustain itself.
- **Satellite economy**: Satellites cost Power permanently. Minimize Satellite use by planning tight building clusters, but don't avoid Federations just to save Power.
- **Opponent denial**: If an opponent needs a specific planet for their Federation, colonizing it first (or nearby) forces them to adapt. Map awareness is critical at higher player counts.
